channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Pope Francis extends prayers, 'cordial greetings' to Trump ahead of inaugural ceremony
item.description: Pope Francis extended his prayers and "cordial greetings" to President-elect Trump ahead of his inaugural ceremony Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Longtime Planned Parenthood President Cecile Richards dies after battle with brain cancer
item.description: Richards, the eldest daughter of Gov. Ann Richards, forged her own path as a tireless advocate for women across Texas and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Eleanor Klibanoff, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump to issue executive orders defining sexes, ending DEI practices in government
item.description: President-elect Trump intends to issue executive orders on his first day in office that will recognize only two sexes, male and female, and aim to end diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices in the federal government, according to incoming White House officials.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Biden pardons Mark Milley, Anthony Fauci, J6 committee members
item.description: President Biden issued pre-emptive pardons for Dr. Anthony Fauci and others who may have faced scrutiny under the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden pardons Fauci, Milley and the Jan. 6 committee in effort to guard against 'revenge' by Trump
item.description: The heads of the Jan_ 6 committee say they're grateful for the decision by President Joe Biden to pardon them “not for breaking the law but for upholding it.”.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Inside Trump's Inauguration Day: How, when and what to watch
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump will take the oath of office at noon followed by a presidential parade, signing ceremony and inaugural balls.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News

item.title: DOJ recommends 20-year sentence for Pennsylvania man convicted for role in Jan. 6 Capitol riot
item.description: The Department of Justice has proposed that a Pennsylvania barber convicted on multiple charges involving the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol riot spend 20 years behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eight years ago, stars avoided Trump's inauguration. This time it’s different
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Carrie Underwood might not be Beyoncé or Garth Brooks in the celebrity superstar ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Moving presidents in, out 'mass bedlam': Former White House usher
item.description: Emery said the process of moving presidents in and out of the White House “is almost like Cirque du Soleil.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's inauguration eve rally
item.description: President-elect Trump on Sunday rallied with supporters in Washington, D.C., on the eve of his second inauguration, taking a victory lap and teasing an expected barrage of activity on his first day in office. Here are five takeaways from Trump’s rally at Capital One Arena.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Live updates: Trump holds victory rally at Capital One Arena, TikTok restores service
item.description: President-elect Trump made the rounds rounds in Washington on Sunday and held a victory rally at Capital One Area just before Inauguration Day. Trump arrived in the nation's capital over the weekend where he is preparing to take a second term in the White House as the Republican trifecta takes form.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's family circle has a different look as he returns to the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Donald Trump returns to the White House on Monday, his family circle will look a little different than it did when he first arrived eight years ago. His youngest son, Barron, was in fifth grade back then.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: These notable inaugurations weren't held at the US Capitol. Here's why
item.description: For decades, Americans have gathered at the Capitol Building in Washington D.C. to watch the inauguration of the incoming president, with some noteworthy exceptions.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Why Trump's tariff promises will be hard to keep
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has said his tariffs will raise revenues, boost U.S. jobs, and help stop the drug trade. But some of these goals are at odds with the others.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: What to watch at Trump's inauguration: Big Tech CEOs, Carrie Underwood and foreign leaders
item.description: Donald Trump's inauguration as the country’s 47th president was expected to be an extravagant break from tradition — before it got moved indoors due to cold weather. There will still be well-known performers, influential billionaires as guests and foreign heads of state.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Then and now: What has changed as Trump returns to the White House
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — When Donald Trump arrived in Washington in 2017 just before his first inauguration, he was a stranger to most in town. As the only president in history without prior experience in public office or the military, it was unclear how he would govern. Not this time.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Clothes in the closets and favorite foods in the fridge: Moving day comes to the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Most presidents get to move into the White House once. President-elect Donald Trump is doing it twice, and his wife, Melania, says it's a lot easier the second time around. “I know where I will be going. I know the rooms where we will be living.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Marco Rubio met with UAP whistleblower prior to Trump nomination
item.description: Barber said he’s worked in both an official and unofficial capacity for the U.S. government and partners in the intelligence community.
item.dc:creator: Ross Coulthart, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands march in Washington days before Trump takes office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Thousands of people from around the United States rallied in the nation's capital Saturday for women's reproductive rights and other causes they believe are under threat from the incoming Trump administration, reprising the original Women's March days before President-elect Donald
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, ASHRAF KHALIL and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judges allow some Capitol riot defendants to return to DC for Trump's inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Thousands of Donald Trump supporters stormed the U.S. Capitol after he lost the 2020 presidential election. Four years later, some of them are allowed to return to the nation's capital so they can celebrate Trump's return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 1985 was the last time an inauguration was indoors. Ronald and Nancy Reagan felt they had no choice
item.description: Ronald and Nancy Reagan were disappointed. That's what White House press secretary Larry Speakes told reporters on Jan. 18, 1985, after the Republican president and first lady decided to hold his second inauguration indoors because of an unusually cold weather forecast.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After major 2024 defeats, the Democratic Party searches for a new direction
item.description: The Democratic Party begins 2025 with several looming questions. Among them: who will lead its national party apparatus, and how it will handle President-elect Donald Trump's second term.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: End to daylight saving time among bills for 2025 Nevada Legislature
item.description: A Republican lawmaker who represents parts of six rural Nevada counties is sponsoring a bill to take the state off daylight saving time — one of the 200 bills written so far.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Capitol chaos: DeSantis faces backlash from Florida lawmakers over special session
item.description: Desantis says the state needs new laws to help President Trump carry out his immigration reforms in Florida. But there's some disagreement over that special session.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
